title: Interpreters and translators
rationale: The core output of this profession—translated text or interpreted speech—is inherently digital or auditory, making it highly susceptible to AI-driven automation. While the work requires high-level cognitive synthesis to capture nuance, tone, and cultural context, the primary barrier to full automation is the systemic accountability required in high-stakes environments (legal, medical) and the ontological necessity of human presence for trust and empathy in sensitive interpersonal interactions.

title: Ironworkers
rationale: The core value of ironworking is the physical manipulation and assembly of heavy structural materials in highly volatile, unstructured, and dangerous environments, which is fundamentally incompatible with current or near-future digital agentic execution. While the work requires spatial reasoning and safety-critical decision-making, the primary barrier to automation is the physical, high-stakes nature of the work, where the human operator bears direct, non-delegatable legal and physical liability for the structural integrity of the build.

title: Janitors and building cleaners
rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical labor in unstructured, real-world environments that require navigating complex, non-standardized spaces, which is highly resistant to current agentic software. While some tasks are routine, the physical variability of building maintenance and the need for on-site, real-time physical intervention keep the agentic output potential extremely low.

title: Jewelers and precious stone and metal workers
rationale: While CAD/CAM and 3D printing allow for digital design, the core value of the occupation remains the physical manipulation, assembly, and repair of precious materials, which requires high-dexterity manual labor that cannot be fully offloaded to software. The environment is highly controlled (workbenches), and while there is a human element in appraisal and custom design, the physical nature of the craft limits the potential for purely agentic, software-based execution.

title: Judges and hearing officers
rationale: While the analytical and research components of the job are highly susceptible to AI, the core output—a binding legal judgment—requires human moral authority and social legitimacy that cannot be delegated to software. The role is defined by systemic accountability and the necessity of a human arbiter to maintain public trust, rendering the agentic output potential low despite the high cognitive complexity of the underlying legal reasoning.

title: Kindergarten and elementary school teachers
rationale: While lesson planning and grading have high digital potential, the core value of the occupation is the real-time, physical, and emotional management of children in an inherently unpredictable classroom environment. The necessity for human presence, moral authority, and the legal/ethical accountability for the development and safety of minors makes this role highly resistant to agentic replacement.

title: Labor relations specialists
rationale: While the drafting of contracts and policy documents is highly amenable to AI-driven generation and analysis, the core value of the role is rooted in high-stakes human mediation, trust-building, and the navigation of volatile interpersonal dynamics. The necessity for a human to hold moral authority and legal accountability during labor disputes and negotiations creates a significant barrier to full automation, despite the high digital output potential of the documentation tasks.

title: Landscape architects
rationale: While the design and modeling phases are increasingly digital and susceptible to AI, the final output requires physical integration into complex, site-specific environments that are inherently unpredictable and require on-site adaptation. Furthermore, the role carries significant legal and safety liability regarding environmental impact, structural integrity, and public safety, necessitating human professional accountability that cannot be fully delegated to an autonomous agent.

title: Lawyers
rationale: While a significant portion of legal drafting and research is increasingly digital and automatable, the core value of the profession remains rooted in high-stakes advocacy, strategic negotiation, and the exercise of moral and legal judgment that requires human presence. The necessity for human accountability in courtrooms and the requirement for trust-based, empathetic client counseling create a high barrier to full agentic replacement, despite the high potential for software to handle the underlying document-based workflows.

title: Librarians and library media specialists
rationale: The core output of a librarian—information retrieval, curation, and research synthesis—is increasingly digital and can be executed by AI agents, leading to high agentic potential. While the role involves human-centric tasks like community programming and pedagogical support, the primary functional value of information management is highly susceptible to automation in structured, low-volatility library environments.

title: Library technicians and assistants
rationale: The core functions of this role—cataloging, database management, and information retrieval—are highly digital and increasingly handled by automated library systems and AI-driven search interfaces, giving it high agentic potential. The work environment is highly structured and predictable, and while human interaction is a component of the service, it does not require the high-stakes moral or professional accountability that would necessitate a human-in-the-loop for every transaction.

title: Licensed practical and licensed vocational nurses
rationale: The core value of this role is physical, bedside care and the human-to-human bond, which cannot be digitized or executed by software. While the role involves monitoring and documentation, the primary tasks—such as physical patient assistance, wound care, and real-time clinical judgment in unpredictable, high-stakes environments—require a human presence and carry significant legal and ethical liability that cannot be delegated to an autonomous agent.

title: Loan officers
rationale: The core output of a loan officer is a digital decision (approval/denial) based on data, which is already heavily mediated by underwriting software and could be fully executed by autonomous agents. While complex commercial loans require human synthesis and relationship management, the primary barrier to full automation is systemic accountability and regulatory compliance rather than physical environmental constraints.

title: Lodging managers
rationale: While revenue management and administrative tasks are increasingly digital, the core of the role involves managing physical facilities and unpredictable human interactions, which limits agentic output potential. The necessity for human presence is high because the job's primary value is providing a hospitable, empathetic, and safe experience for guests in a dynamic, physical environment.

title: Logging workers
rationale: The core value of logging is the physical extraction and processing of timber in highly volatile, unstructured, and dangerous outdoor environments, which remains largely resistant to autonomous digital execution. While some data-entry tasks exist for graders, the primary work involves navigating complex physical terrain and operating heavy machinery, making the agentic potential extremely low.

title: Logisticians
rationale: The core output of a logistician is digital—plans, schedules, and procurement commands—which are increasingly executed directly by supply chain management software, giving this role high agentic potential. While the work involves managing physical goods, the logistician operates primarily through data-driven synthesis and system optimization rather than direct physical manipulation, allowing AI to handle the vast majority of the decision-making loop.

title: Machinists and tool and die makers
rationale: While the design phase (CAD/CAM) is digital, the core value of this occupation is the physical transformation of raw materials into precision parts, which requires physical manipulation and real-world verification that software cannot currently execute end-to-end. The work involves significant systemic accountability, as errors in precision components can lead to catastrophic mechanical failure in high-stakes industries like aerospace or medical manufacturing.

title: Management analysts
rationale: The core output of a management analyst is a digital artifact—reports, strategic recommendations, and process models—which are highly susceptible to AI generation and execution. While the work requires high-level synthesis of organizational dynamics, the physical environment is generally controlled, and the primary 'human' necessity lies in the social capital, trust, and political navigation required to implement changes within a human-led organization.

title: Manicurists and pedicurists
rationale: The core value of this occupation is a physical, tactile service performed on a human body, which cannot be digitized or executed by software agents. While the work environment is relatively controlled, the necessity for human touch, interpersonal connection, and the physical dexterity required to safely manipulate sharp tools around a client's skin makes this role highly resistant to automation.

title: Marine engineers and naval architects
rationale: While the design and schematic phases are increasingly digital, the core value of this role involves physical integration with complex, high-stakes maritime environments where AI cannot yet navigate the physical unpredictability of ship construction and offshore operations. The high systemic accountability score reflects the life-critical nature of marine engineering, where errors in structural integrity or safety systems carry immense legal and ethical liability that cannot be delegated to an autonomous agent.

title: Market research analysts
rationale: The core output of this role—data analysis, trend forecasting, and report generation—is entirely digital and increasingly executable by AI agents that can process big data sets autonomously. While the role requires high-level synthesis, the work environment is highly controlled and digital, leaving little room for physical unpredictability or the necessity of a human-to-human bond for the primary product.

title: Marriage and family therapists
rationale: The core value of this profession is rooted in the human-to-human therapeutic alliance, which cannot be replicated by digital agents regardless of cognitive sophistication. While administrative tasks are automatable, the primary output—emotional healing and behavioral change within complex family systems—requires a human presence, moral accountability, and the ability to navigate the volatile, unstructured emotional realities of clients.

title: Masonry workers
rationale: Masonry is a fundamentally physical trade requiring real-time interaction with unstructured, variable environments (e.g., uneven surfaces, weather, site-specific constraints) that cannot be digitized or executed by software. While the cognitive aspects of planning are complex, the core value is the physical manipulation of materials, and the high systemic accountability stems from the critical safety and structural integrity requirements of the built environment.

title: Massage therapists
rationale: The core value of massage therapy is the physical, tactile manipulation of human soft tissue, which cannot be digitized or executed by software. While the profession requires diagnostic assessment and personalized treatment planning, the primary product is a human-to-human interaction rooted in physical presence, empathy, and the trust required for intimate, hands-on care.

title: Material moving machine operators
rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical manipulation of heavy objects in real-world environments, which cannot be executed by software alone. While the tasks are procedural, the high-stakes physical environment—such as operating cranes near workers or navigating construction sites—requires human-in-the-loop safety and liability, making full autonomous delegation difficult and legally fraught.

title: Material recording clerks
rationale: The core value of this role is information management and inventory tracking, which is increasingly handled by digital systems, RFID, and automated software, giving it high agentic potential. While the work occurs in physical warehouses, the tasks are largely procedural and standardized, requiring minimal complex synthesis or human-to-human emotional labor, making the role highly susceptible to full digital and robotic automation.

title: Materials engineers
rationale: While materials engineers use digital tools for simulation, their core value lies in the physical validation and deployment of materials in complex, often unpredictable real-world environments where failure can be catastrophic. Because they are responsible for the safety and integrity of physical structures and products, the final decision-making and legal liability remain firmly human, preventing full delegation to autonomous agents.

title: Mathematicians and statisticians
rationale: The core output of this profession is digital—models, reports, and code—which is highly susceptible to automation by AI agents capable of end-to-end data processing and synthesis. While the work involves high-level cognitive complexity and significant systemic accountability for the validity of models and their real-world consequences, the environment is almost exclusively controlled and digital, requiring minimal physical interaction.

title: Mechanical engineering technologists and technicians
rationale: While the design and documentation aspects of this role are increasingly digital and susceptible to AI, the core value lies in the physical assembly, installation, and troubleshooting of mechanical systems in varied, often unstructured industrial environments. Because the work requires physical interaction with hardware and real-world testing where variables are not perfectly predictable, it remains resistant to full agentic automation despite the high cognitive requirements for technical problem-solving.

title: Mechanical engineers
rationale: While mechanical engineers use digital tools for design and simulation, their core output is the physical realization of machines that must function reliably in the real world, which cannot be fully delegated to software. The high complexity of integrating thermal, mechanical, and electronic systems, combined with the severe legal and safety liabilities (PE licensure) associated with physical infrastructure and machinery, ensures that human oversight remains critical for final validation and accountability.

title: Medical and health services managers
rationale: While much of the administrative and analytical output (budgeting, scheduling, reporting) is highly susceptible to digital automation, the role remains anchored in the management of human teams and the navigation of high-stakes, real-world clinical environments. The necessity for human leadership, ethical oversight in patient care, and legal accountability for facility-wide outcomes creates a significant barrier to full agentic replacement.

title: Medical assistants
rationale: While administrative tasks like scheduling and data entry have high agentic potential, the core of the role involves physical, in-person clinical interaction—such as drawing blood, taking vitals, and providing compassionate care—which cannot be digitized. The necessity for human presence and the high-stakes nature of patient safety and medical confidentiality create significant barriers to full automation, despite the routine nature of many individual tasks.

title: Medical dosimetrists
rationale: The core output of a dosimetrist is a digital treatment plan (a set of coordinates and dosage parameters) that is directly executable by radiation delivery software, giving it extremely high agentic potential. While the work requires high-level synthesis of complex medical data, the environment is highly controlled and clinical, minimizing physical unpredictability. However, the role remains tethered to human oversight due to the extreme, life-altering consequences of radiation errors, which necessitate non-delegatable professional and ethical accountability.

title: Medical equipment repairers
rationale: While software diagnostics and calibration are increasingly automated, the core value of this role lies in physical, on-site manipulation of complex, life-critical hardware in unpredictable clinical environments. The high systemic accountability score reflects the life-or-death consequences of equipment failure, which necessitates human liability and physical intervention that cannot be delegated to autonomous agents.

title: Medical records specialists
rationale: The core output of this role is digital data entry and classification, which is highly susceptible to automation via AI agents that can interpret clinical notes and assign standardized codes. While the role requires accuracy and adherence to privacy laws, the work occurs in a highly controlled, digital environment, making it a prime candidate for end-to-end software execution.

title: Medical scientists
rationale: While medical scientists produce digital data and research papers, the core value is tied to physical laboratory experimentation and clinical trials that require physical manipulation and human oversight. The high complexity and accountability scores reflect the life-altering nature of medical breakthroughs and the ethical, non-delegatable responsibility for patient safety and scientific integrity in high-stakes research environments.

title: Medical transcriptionists
rationale: The core output of this role is entirely digital and already heavily automated by speech-to-text software, making it a prime candidate for full agentic execution. The work is performed in highly controlled, virtual environments with minimal physical variables, and the human role is primarily that of a quality-control auditor rather than a source of unique human value.

title: Meeting, convention, and event planners
rationale: While much of the administrative and logistical planning (scheduling, budgeting, vendor communication) can be digitized and automated, the core value of the role lies in on-site crisis management and the high-touch, empathetic negotiation required to satisfy human clients. The physical environment of an event is inherently volatile and unstructured, requiring real-time human judgment and social presence that software cannot replicate.

title: Metal and plastic machine workers
rationale: The core value of this occupation is the physical transformation of raw materials into tangible goods, which cannot be executed by software alone regardless of how much the process is digitized. While the work environment is generally controlled, it involves high-stakes physical variables like heavy machinery, molten materials, and hazardous conditions that require human presence for safety monitoring and physical intervention.

title: Microbiologists
rationale: While AI can assist with data analysis and reporting, the core value of a microbiologist lies in physical laboratory experimentation, sample handling, and the interpretation of biological phenomena that cannot be fully digitized or automated. The high systemic accountability score reflects the critical nature of their work in public health, drug development, and environmental safety, where human oversight is required to validate findings and assume liability for life-altering scientific conclusions.

title: Middle school teachers
rationale: While lesson planning and grading have high digital potential, the core value of middle school teaching is the real-time, in-person social and emotional development of adolescents, which cannot be delegated to software. The classroom environment is highly unpredictable, requiring constant, high-stakes human judgment and moral authority that remains fundamentally tied to the human-to-human bond.

title: Military careers
rationale: Military operations are fundamentally defined by physical, high-stakes, and unpredictable environments where the primary output is the projection of force or physical security, which cannot be delegated to software. The necessity for human moral authority, ethical judgment in lethal scenarios, and the weight of systemic accountability for life-and-death decisions make this occupation highly resistant to AI-driven automation.

title: Mining and geological engineers
rationale: While mining engineers produce digital designs and reports, the core value is the physical implementation of complex, high-stakes infrastructure in volatile, unstructured geological environments where AI cannot yet manage the physical risks. The high systemic accountability score reflects the non-delegatable legal and ethical liability for worker safety and environmental integrity, which requires human professional judgment that cannot be offloaded to an autonomous agent.

title: Models
rationale: The core output of modeling—a visual representation of a product or aesthetic—is increasingly digital and can be synthesized by generative AI, leading to high agentic potential. While live runway or in-person retail work requires physical presence, the vast majority of the industry's value is derived from the final image or video, which is highly susceptible to automation.

title: Music directors and composers
rationale: While the composition of music is increasingly digital and highly susceptible to AI-driven generation, the role of a music director remains deeply rooted in the human-to-human interaction required to lead, inspire, and synchronize live performers. The high cognitive complexity reflects the nuanced, non-routine nature of artistic interpretation, which, while potentially replicable in output, requires a human presence to maintain the social and emotional contract of a live performance.

title: Musicians and singers
rationale: While AI can generate high-quality musical artifacts, the core value of this occupation is the 'live' human performance and the shared physical experience between performer and audience, which cannot be replaced by digital output. The high cognitive complexity stems from the creative synthesis required for artistic expression, but the role lacks systemic accountability as the stakes are primarily cultural and aesthetic rather than life-critical or legal.

title: Natural sciences managers
rationale: While much of the reporting and data analysis can be digitized, the core value of this role lies in human leadership, team motivation, and the high-stakes ethical oversight of scientific research. The requirement for physical presence in laboratories and field sites, combined with the non-delegatable responsibility for safety and scientific integrity, limits the potential for full agentic automation.

title: Network and computer systems administrators
rationale: The core output of this role is entirely digital—managing configurations, permissions, and system architecture—which is highly susceptible to autonomous agent execution via Infrastructure-as-Code and automated orchestration. While the work requires complex synthesis to design and troubleshoot systems, the environment is largely controlled and virtualized, leaving little room for human-centric necessity beyond high-level accountability for system-critical failures.

title: News analysts, reporters, and journalists
rationale: The core output of journalism is digital information, which is highly susceptible to AI generation and automated distribution. While the job involves physical presence in unpredictable environments for investigative reporting and relationship building, the final product—the news story or analysis—is increasingly a synthesis task that software can execute end-to-end.

title: Nuclear engineers
rationale: While nuclear engineers utilize digital modeling and simulation, their core output involves physical safety, regulatory compliance, and the management of high-stakes, volatile nuclear environments where AI cannot physically intervene or assume liability. The necessity for human judgment in managing catastrophic risk and the legal requirement for professional accountability ensure that the human role remains central despite the high degree of technical complexity.

title: Nuclear medicine technologists
rationale: While the diagnostic output is digital, the core value of the role involves the physical, high-stakes administration of radioactive materials to human patients, which cannot be delegated to software. The requirement for real-time patient monitoring, physical assistance, and the severe legal and ethical liability associated with radiation safety and medical intervention necessitates a human presence that AI cannot currently replace.

title: Nuclear technicians
rationale: While data monitoring and analysis are increasingly digital, the core value of this role lies in physical maintenance, onsite sample collection, and emergency response within high-stakes, volatile nuclear environments that require human physical presence. The extreme systemic accountability for safety and the physical nature of the work environment create significant barriers to full agentic automation, as the 'final step' involves physical intervention in hazardous, non-standardized settings.

title: Nurse anesthetists, nurse midwives, and nurse practitioners
rationale: The core value of this role is physical, in-person clinical intervention and patient care, which cannot be digitized or executed by software agents. While the cognitive synthesis required for diagnosis and treatment planning is high, the work is fundamentally anchored in high-stakes, unpredictable physical environments where human presence, physical touch, and legal/ethical accountability for life-altering decisions are non-delegatable.

title: Nursing assistants and orderlies
rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical, in-person care and patient interaction, which cannot be digitized or executed via software agents. The work environment is highly unpredictable due to the physical variability of patients and the need for real-time, empathetic human presence, making it fundamentally resistant to automation despite the routine nature of some tasks.

title: Occupational health and safety specialists and technicians
rationale: While AI can analyze data and generate reports, the core value of this role lies in physical site inspections, real-world hazard mitigation, and the enforcement of safety protocols in volatile, unstructured environments like construction sites or mines. The high systemic accountability score reflects the legal and ethical burden of ensuring human life safety, which requires a human agent to exercise professional judgment and assume liability for site-specific risks that software cannot legally or physically own.

title: Occupational therapists
rationale: The core value of occupational therapy is physical, in-person rehabilitation and the development of a therapeutic human bond, which cannot be digitized or executed by software. While the cognitive synthesis required to design treatment plans is high, the execution is inherently tied to the physical, unpredictable, and high-stakes environment of patient care, where human empathy and legal accountability for patient safety are non-delegatable.

title: Occupational therapy assistants and aides
rationale: The core value of this role is physical, in-person therapeutic intervention and patient support, which cannot be digitized or executed via software. The work environment is highly unpredictable due to the physical variability of patients and the need for real-time, empathetic human interaction, making the human presence an essential component of the service rather than a replaceable interface.

title: Oil and gas workers
rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical labor, equipment maintenance, and extraction in highly volatile, unstructured, and dangerous environments, which cannot be replaced by digital agents. While cognitive tasks like monitoring and troubleshooting exist, the primary output is the physical manipulation of heavy machinery and raw materials, necessitating human presence for safety and real-time physical intervention.

title: Operations research analysts
rationale: The core output of an operations research analyst is a digital model, report, or algorithmic recommendation, which is highly susceptible to end-to-end execution by AI agents. While the role requires high-level synthesis of complex variables, the work environment is almost entirely controlled and digital, leaving little room for physical unpredictability that would hinder automation.

title: Opticians
rationale: While the ordering and data entry aspects of the job are highly digitizable, the core value of an optician lies in the physical, in-person manipulation of eyewear to fit a specific human face and the interpersonal guidance provided to the customer. The physical nature of fitting, adjusting, and the necessity of human-to-human trust in a healthcare-adjacent retail environment limits the potential for full agentic replacement.

title: Optometrists
rationale: While diagnostic data and prescription generation are increasingly digital, the core value of optometry remains the physical examination, minor surgical intervention, and the human-centric delivery of care. The high systemic accountability and the necessity for human trust in clinical decision-making and patient counseling act as significant barriers to full agentic automation.

title: Orthotists and prosthetists
rationale: While digital design and 3D printing are increasingly used, the core value remains the physical, high-stakes integration of a device with a unique, living human body, which requires manual dexterity and real-time physical adjustments. The occupation carries significant clinical liability and requires deep empathy and trust, as the practitioner is directly responsible for the patient's mobility, comfort, and long-term physical health.

title: Painters, construction and maintenance
rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical labor—modifying the material state of structures—which cannot be executed by software or digital agents. The work environment is highly unstructured, involving complex physical navigation, heights, and variable site conditions that remain difficult for autonomous systems to manage reliably.

title: Painting and coating workers
rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical manipulation of materials in a 3D space, which is inherently resistant to digital agentic execution despite the use of automated machinery. While some tasks involve machine operation, the requirement for manual touch-ups, surface preparation, and handling of irregular objects in physical environments keeps the agentic potential low and the reliance on human physical presence high.

title: Paralegals and legal assistants
rationale: The core output of a paralegal—legal research, document drafting, and case organization—is almost entirely digital and highly susceptible to end-to-end execution by AI agents. While the work requires high-level synthesis of legal information, it occurs in a highly controlled office environment, and the ultimate legal liability and ethical responsibility remain with the attorney, limiting the necessity for human-in-the-loop intervention for the majority of tasks.

title: Personal financial advisors
rationale: The core output of this role is digital financial strategy and transaction execution, which is highly susceptible to agentic automation. While the technical aspects of portfolio management are increasingly algorithmic, the role maintains high scores in human necessity and accountability due to the psychological need for trust, behavioral coaching, and the legal liability associated with managing life-altering assets.

title: Pest control workers
rationale: The core value of this job is physical intervention in highly variable, unstructured environments (crawlspaces, attics, and diverse building structures) that require human dexterity and real-time physical judgment. While the planning aspect is cognitive, the execution is inherently physical and high-stakes due to the use of toxic chemicals, necessitating human accountability for safety and regulatory compliance.

title: Petroleum engineers
rationale: While petroleum engineers perform complex data analysis that AI can assist with, their core value lies in high-stakes physical engineering decisions in volatile, unstructured environments like offshore rigs or deep-well sites where AI cannot physically intervene. The role carries immense systemic accountability for safety and environmental impact, requiring human professional judgment and legal liability that cannot be delegated to software.

title: Pharmacists
rationale: While the dispensing of medication is increasingly automated, the core value of the pharmacist lies in high-stakes clinical judgment, drug-interaction synthesis, and the human-centric delivery of care and patient education. The occupation carries profound legal and ethical liability for patient safety that cannot be delegated to an agent, and the necessity for human empathy and trust in a clinical setting remains a critical, non-automatable component of the role.

title: Pharmacy technicians
rationale: While much of the workflow involves digital data entry and insurance processing—tasks highly susceptible to automation—the physical handling, measuring, and verification of medication in a clinical setting limits full agentic potential. The role carries high systemic accountability due to the life-altering consequences of medication errors, necessitating human oversight even as the physical and administrative tasks become increasingly automated.

title: Phlebotomists
rationale: The core value of this job is a physical, invasive medical procedure that requires high-precision manual dexterity and real-time adjustment to patient physiology, which cannot be delegated to software. While data entry is digital, the primary output is the successful collection of a biological sample, and the human-to-human interaction is critical for managing patient anxiety and ensuring safety in a clinical environment.

title: Photographers
rationale: While photography involves physical presence and environmental navigation, the core output is increasingly digital, and generative AI is rapidly encroaching on the synthesis of aesthetic composition and post-production editing. The role retains a moderate human necessity for client interaction and directing subjects, but the technical execution of the final image is highly susceptible to digital automation.

title: Physical therapist assistants and aides
rationale: The core value of this role is physical, hands-on patient care and manual therapy, which cannot be digitized or executed by software agents. While the work involves following a plan of care, the physical interaction with patients in varying states of health requires high levels of human empathy and real-time physical judgment that remains fundamentally human-centric.

title: Physical therapists
rationale: The core value of physical therapy is rooted in physical, hands-on manipulation and the human-to-human therapeutic bond, which cannot be digitized or delegated to software. While the cognitive synthesis required to diagnose and adapt treatment plans is high, the execution remains a physical, high-stakes interaction that requires human accountability for patient safety and recovery outcomes.

title: Physician assistants
rationale: While AI can assist with diagnostics and data synthesis, the core output of a PA involves physical interventions—such as suturing, setting bones, and physical examinations—which cannot be executed by software. The role is deeply anchored in human-to-human trust, physical presence in unpredictable clinical environments, and high-stakes legal and ethical accountability for patient outcomes that cannot be delegated to an autonomous agent.

title: Physicians and surgeons
rationale: While AI can assist with diagnostic synthesis, the core output of a physician or surgeon is a physical intervention on a biological system, which cannot be delegated to software. The high scores in environmental unpredictability and ontological necessity reflect the reality that medical practice involves navigating complex, non-standardized biological variables and the fundamental requirement for human trust, empathy, and legal accountability in life-or-death decision-making.

title: Physicists and astronomers
rationale: While AI can assist with data analysis and modeling, the core value of this profession lies in the physical design, calibration, and operation of experimental apparatuses and the interpretation of novel phenomena, which remain tethered to physical reality. The high systemic accountability score reflects the critical nature of their work in fields like medical physics, nuclear energy, and national security, where human oversight is essential for safety and ethical validation.

title: Plumbers, pipefitters, and steamfitters
rationale: The core value of this occupation is physical manipulation of infrastructure in highly unstructured, often cramped, and volatile environments, which remains largely beyond the reach of current agentic software. While planning and diagnostics involve complex synthesis, the final execution requires real-world dexterity and high-stakes accountability for safety and building code compliance that cannot be delegated to a digital agent.

title: Podiatrists
rationale: The core value of podiatry is physical, invasive intervention and surgery on human anatomy, which cannot be digitized or executed by software agents. While AI can assist in diagnostic synthesis, the physical unpredictability of patient physiology and the high-stakes legal and ethical accountability for surgical outcomes necessitate a human practitioner at every stage of the process.

title: Police and detectives
rationale: The core value of police work is physical intervention, presence, and the exercise of state-sanctioned authority in highly volatile, unstructured environments, which cannot be delegated to software. While data analysis and report writing are cognitive tasks, the final output—maintaining public order and making life-altering decisions—requires human moral agency, physical presence, and non-delegatable legal accountability.

title: Political scientists
rationale: The core output of a political scientist—reports, policy forecasts, and data-driven strategic insights—is entirely digital and highly susceptible to automation by advanced AI agents capable of synthesizing vast datasets. While the work requires high-level cognitive synthesis to navigate complex political systems, the environment is largely controlled and information-based, minimizing the need for physical presence or human-to-human emotional labor as a primary product.

title: Postal service workers
rationale: The core value of this occupation is the physical movement and delivery of tangible goods, which remains largely outside the reach of digital-only agentic execution. While sorting and processing are increasingly automated, the 'last mile' delivery involves navigating unstructured, real-world environments and physical handling that requires human dexterity and adaptability.

title: Postsecondary education administrators
rationale: Much of the administrative work—such as scheduling, record-keeping, and data analysis—is highly digital and ripe for agentic automation. However, the role remains anchored by the necessity for human-to-human interaction in student advising, conflict resolution, and high-stakes institutional decision-making (e.g., tenure and admissions policy), where human judgment and ethical accountability are non-delegatable.

title: Postsecondary teachers
rationale: While course materials and administrative tasks have high agentic potential, the core value of postsecondary teaching remains deeply rooted in human mentorship, moral authority, and the facilitation of complex, non-linear intellectual development. The environment is highly structured, but the necessity for human-to-human connection and the ethical responsibility for student growth and academic integrity create significant barriers to full automation.

title: Power plant operators, distributors, and dispatchers
rationale: The core output of this role is the digital manipulation of grid and plant controls, which is increasingly being integrated into automated software systems, leading to a high agentic potential. However, the role remains anchored by extreme systemic accountability, particularly in nuclear and grid-critical environments, where human oversight is legally and ethically required to manage high-stakes, life-altering failure scenarios that machines cannot yet fully own.

title: Preschool and childcare center directors
rationale: While administrative tasks like budgeting and scheduling have some digital output potential, the core of the role involves managing the volatile, high-stakes physical environment of a childcare facility. The necessity for human presence is absolute, as the job centers on the emotional, developmental, and physical safety of children, creating a non-delegatable duty of care that AI cannot fulfill.

title: Preschool teachers
rationale: The core value of this occupation is the physical, emotional, and social development of children, which requires a constant, in-person human presence that cannot be replaced by digital artifacts. While curriculum planning involves cognitive synthesis, the execution is entirely dependent on managing the highly unpredictable, real-time physical environment of a classroom, making the role fundamentally resistant to agentic automation.

title: Private detectives and investigators
rationale: While digital OSINT and data aggregation are highly automatable, the core of the profession relies on physical surveillance, in-person interviews, and navigating unpredictable, real-world environments where human intuition and presence are required. Furthermore, the role carries significant legal and ethical liability regarding privacy and evidence admissibility, which necessitates human accountability that cannot be offloaded to an autonomous agent.

title: Probation officers and correctional treatment specialists
rationale: While reporting and data management are digital, the core value of this role is physical supervision, in-person assessment of volatile environments, and the exercise of human authority in high-stakes legal contexts. The necessity for human presence to build trust, manage physical safety, and accept legal liability for life-altering decisions makes this role highly resistant to full agentic automation.

title: Producers and directors
rationale: While the final product (film/media) is digital, the core value of the role lies in the high-stakes, real-world coordination of human talent, physical sets, and volatile on-location environments that AI cannot yet navigate. The job requires deep creative synthesis and the ability to manage complex human dynamics, making the 'human-in-the-loop' element essential for both the artistic vision and the liability of large-scale production management.

title: Project management specialists
rationale: Project management is highly susceptible to AI because the core outputs—schedules, budgets, status reports, and resource allocation plans—are digital artifacts that can be generated and executed by autonomous agents. While the role requires high-level synthesis and human-centric negotiation to manage stakeholder expectations and team dynamics, the technical execution of project tracking and documentation is increasingly within the domain of software-driven automation.

title: Property appraisers and assessors
rationale: While the physical inspection of property remains a hurdle, the core value of the job—the valuation report—is a digital artifact that is increasingly generated by automated valuation models (AVMs). The high score for systemic accountability reflects the legal and financial weight of these valuations, which serve as the basis for mortgages and tax assessments, requiring a human to sign off on the liability despite the high potential for AI-driven execution.

title: Property, real estate, and community association managers
rationale: While administrative tasks like billing and scheduling have high agentic potential, the core of the role involves navigating unpredictable physical environments (maintenance issues, property inspections) and managing complex human conflicts that require empathy and social intelligence. The necessity for human presence to mediate disputes, enforce rules, and provide physical oversight of assets limits the extent to which an AI agent can fully replace the role.

title: Psychiatric technicians and aides
rationale: The core value of this role is physical, in-person care, patient monitoring, and crisis intervention, which cannot be digitized or executed via software. The environment is highly unpredictable due to the potential for patient violence and the need for real-time, empathetic human judgment, making the role fundamentally resistant to AI-driven automation.

title: Psychologists
rationale: While AI can assist in data analysis and diagnostic pattern recognition, the core value of psychology is rooted in the therapeutic alliance, empathy, and the ethical responsibility of human-to-human connection, which cannot be delegated to software. The work involves navigating the highly unpredictable and nuanced emotional states of human clients, requiring a level of moral and professional accountability that remains fundamentally human.

title: Public relations and fundraising managers
rationale: While much of the output (press releases, campaign strategy, digital content) is highly amenable to AI generation, the core value of these roles relies on high-stakes human relationship management, trust-building with donors, and the navigation of sensitive, reputation-critical crises. The necessity for human presence in high-value fundraising and the requirement for human accountability in legal and ethical communications limit the extent to which these roles can be fully delegated to autonomous agents.

title: Public relations specialists
rationale: The core output of this role—press releases, social media content, and strategic messaging—is entirely digital and can be generated and disseminated by AI agents. While the job requires high-level synthesis of public sentiment and organizational strategy, the work environment is highly controlled and office-based, making it a prime candidate for automation despite the residual need for human-to-human trust in high-stakes crisis management.

title: Public safety telecommunicators
rationale: The core output of this role is the digital dispatch of resources and the relaying of information, which is highly amenable to AI execution. However, the role maintains a high score in systemic accountability and ontological human necessity because the dispatcher serves as the critical moral and legal bridge between a panicked caller and life-saving intervention, where human empathy and the assumption of liability for life-altering decisions remain essential.

title: Purchasing managers, buyers, and purchasing agents
rationale: The core output of this role—contracts, purchase orders, and supply chain logistics—is inherently digital and highly amenable to end-to-end execution by autonomous agents. title: Quality control inspectors
rationale: The core function of this role—inspecting, measuring, and reporting on product defects—is increasingly handled by automated vision systems and digital sensors, giving it high agentic potential. While the work occurs in physical manufacturing environments, the task itself is highly procedural and relies on objective, quantifiable data rather than complex human synthesis or interpersonal interaction.

title: Radiation therapists
rationale: While the operation of the linear accelerator is technical, the core value is the physical positioning of a patient and the real-time monitoring of their physiological and emotional state, which cannot be delegated to software. The high systemic accountability score reflects the life-critical nature of radiation delivery, where human oversight is legally and ethically required to prevent catastrophic medical errors.

title: Radiologic and MRI technologists
rationale: While the final output (the image) is digital, the core value of the role is the physical positioning, safety monitoring, and stabilization of human patients, which cannot be delegated to software. title: Recreation workers

title: Recreational therapists

rationale: The core value of nursing is rooted in physical, real-world patient care and the irreplaceable human-to-human bond, making the agentic output potential extremely low despite the high cognitive complexity of clinical decision-making.
